title: Operations methods: job, batch, flow, mass customisation
summary: 9609 AS — job, batch, flow, and mass customisation production methods with examples and evaluation.
learningObjectives: Describe job, batch, flow, and mass customisation methods.
Compare unit costs, flexibility, and setup times.
Select appropriate method for case products.
Evaluate mass customisation as a hybrid approach.

simpleExplanation.title: How production is organised
simpleExplanation.summary: Job production makes one-off items; batch makes groups; flow is continuous high volume; mass customisation uses flexible tech to offer variety at near-mass-production cost.
steps: Job: unique products, skilled labour, high unit cost.
Batch: groups between changeovers, moderate cost.
Flow: assembly line, standardised, lowest unit cost.
Mass customisation: flexible automation (e.g. Dell PCs).

flashcards.back: Bespoke wedding cake, custom bridge engineering.
Car assembly line, soft drinks bottling.
Customer chooses options; flexible manufacturing builds to order.
Job — each order reconfigured; flow has low per-unit setup.
Job, then batch; flow least flexible.
Flow often needs high finished goods stock; job = made to order.

sections.content: **Operations methods** (4.1.4) — match **method to product life cycle and market**. Examiners love **justify your choice** with **cost, flexibility, and customer needs**.

items: **Job:** One-off, high skill, highest unit cost, maximum customisation.
**Batch:** Shared setup per group; balance of cost and variety.
**Flow:** Continuous, capital intensive, lowest unit cost, limited variety.
**Mass customisation:** IT + flexible machinery; premium for personalisation.

sections.question: Furniture firm makes standard chairs (high volume) and custom office fit-outs (low volume). Recommend production methods for each.
sections.solution:
**Standard chairs:** **Flow or batch** — predictable demand, **low unit cost** competes on price; inventory of popular models.

**Custom fit-outs:** **Job production** — unique specs, **site measurement**, skilled joiners; quote per project.

**Optional hybrid:** **Mass customisation** for chair fabrics/colours online while frame is batch-built — links **3.3.4 product** variety with **efficient** base production.
